items.docs: Returns all asset groups.

Creates a new asset group. The `searchCriteria` field can be passed no matter what the type of the asset group is. The asset group `type` changes when the assets are refreshed. Dynamic asset groups constantly refreshed their membership as assets are scanned whereas static asset groups do not change membership automatically. See the Search Criteria for more information on using dynamic criteria.

Returns all available report formats. A report format indicates an output file format specification (e.g. PDF, XML, etc). Some printable formats may be templated, and others may not. The supported templates for each formated are provided.

items.docs: Returns all defined report configurations.

Configures a new report for generation. Report types are controlled through either or both a format and template. Non-templatized (`export`) report formats do not require a template and have their output format preset. Templatized (`document` and `file`) report formats support a report template that governs the content of the output and the output format can be chosen from a list of supported formats.
Returns the configuration details of a report.
Updates the configuration details of a report.
Deletes the configuration of a report.
Generates a configured report and returns the instance identifier of the report.
Returns all historical details for generation of the report over time.

Returns the current valid shared secret, if one has been previously generated and it has not yet expired; otherwise the endpoint will respond with a 404 status code. Use this endpoint to detect whether a previously-generated shared secret is still valid.

Returns the current valid shared secret or generates a new shared secret. The endpoint returns an existing shared secret if one was previously generated and it has not yet expired. Conversely, the endpoint will generate and return a new shared secret for either of the following conditions: a shared secret was not previously generated or the previously-generated shared secret has expired. The shared secret is valid for 60 minutes from the moment it is generated.
Revokes the current valid shared secret, if one exists.

info.name: Scan Engine Shared Secret Time to live

items.docs: Returns the number of seconds remaining for the current shared secret before it expires, if one has been previously generated and it has not yet expired; otherwise the endpoint will respond with a 404 status code.

items.docs: Returns all tags.
Creates a new tag.
Returns a tag.
Updates the details of a tag. For more information about accepted fields for the tag search criteria see the PUT /search_criteria documentation.
Deletes the tag.
Returns the asset groups associated with the tag.
Sets the asset groups associated with the tag.
Removes the associations between the tag and all asset groups.
Adds an asset group to this tag.
Removes an asset group from this tag.
Returns the assets tagged with a tag.
Adds an asset to the tag.
Removes an asset from the tag. Note: The asset must be added through the asset or tag, if the asset is added using a site, asset group, or search criteria this will not remove the asset.
Returns the search criteria associated with the tag.

Updates the search criteria associated with the tag. 

The following table outlines the search criteria fields and the available operators: 
| Field | Operators | 
| ---------- | ---------------- | 
| ip-address | is, is-not, in-range, not-in-range, is-like, not-like | 
| ip-address-type | in, not-in | 
| alternate-address-type | in | 
| host-name | is, is-not, starts-with, ends-with, contains, does-not-contain, is-empty, is-not-empty, is-like, not-like | 
| host-type | in, not-in | 
| operating
Removes the search criteria associated with the tag.
Returns the sites associated with the tag.
Sets the sites associated with the tag.
Removes the associations between the tag and the sites.
Adds a site to this tag.
Removes a site from this tag.
Returns the vulnerability checks that assess for a specific vulnerability during a scan.

params.name: search
params.description: Vulnerability search term to find vulnerability checks for. e.g. `"ssh"`.

params.name: safe
params.description: Whether to return vulnerability checks that are considered "safe" to run. Defaults to return safe and unsafe checks.

params.name: potential
params.description: Whether to only return checks that result in potentially vulnerable results. Defaults to return all checks.

params.name: requiresCredentials
params.description: Whether to only return checks that require credentials in order to successfully execute. Defaults to return all checks.

params.name: unique
params.description: Whether to only return checks that guarantee to be executed once-and-only once on a host resulting in a unique result. False returns checks that can result in multiple occurrences of the same vulnerability on a host.

params.name: type
params.description: The type of vulnerability checks to return. See Check Types for all available types.

params.name: page
params.description: The index of the page (zero-based) to retrieve.

params.name: size
params.description: The number of records per page to retrieve.

params.name: sort
params.description: The criteria to sort the records by, in the format: `property[,ASC|DESC]`. The default sort order is ascending. Multiple sort criteria can be specified using multiple sort query parameters.

items.docs: Returns vulnerability checks. Optional search and filtering parameters may be supplied to refine the results. Searching allows full text search of the vulnerability details a check is related to.

params.name: id
params.description: The identifier of the vulnerability check.

items.docs: Returns the vulnerability check.
Returns the vulnerability check types. The type groups related vulnerability checks by their purpose, property, or related characteristic.

params.description: The index of the page (zero-based) to retrieve.

items.docs: Returns all exceptions defined on vulnerabilities.
Creates a vulnerability exception.

params.name: id
params.description: The identifier of the vulnerability exception.

items.docs: Returns an exception made on a vulnerability.
Removes an exception made on a vulnerability.
Get the expiration date for a vulnerability exception.
Set the expiration date for a vulnerability exception. This must be a valid date in the future.
Update the status of the vulnerability exception. The status can be one of: `"recall"`, `"approve"`, or `"reject"`.
